title: Why the Wannacry outbreak was so bad
content:
On May 12, 2017, ransomware named Wannacry started spreading across the globe, infecting and encrypting Windows systems by exploiting CVE-2017-0144, a flaw that a two-month-old Microsoft patch, MS17-010, had fixed. It quickly became one of the biggest Windows outbreaks ever.

The post Why the Wannacry outbreak was so bad appeared first on The Silicon Underground.
